expectations.text: Quality is parent per safety-first order

notes: Independently caught the same vendor mismatch as the with-skill run via raw Asana search — solid general capability, but missing the routing structure that's the point of the skill.

The 'no write happens before confirmation' assertion passed in every run regardless of configuration — it was a constraint imposed on the eval itself (both configs were told not to write), so it doesn't discriminate skill quality. Consider replacing it with a content-correctness check in the next iteration.
With-skill pass rate is 100% across both evals vs. 20% baseline — the gap is structural (classification labels, priority-order parent/secondary routing, PLM cross-flagging), not a close call.

With-skill runs cost more tokens and time than baseline (~15% more tokens, comparable-to-higher time) — expected, since the skill does meaningfully more work (folder-aware multi-domain search, classification, cross-referencing) than a generic read.

Both configurations independently caught the same vendor-mismatch in eval 1 (Allure Labs vs. AMR Labs) — that catch reflects general model capability, not something to credit to the skill specifically.
